events.title: Three-vessel collision spills fuel oil at mouth of Tampa Bay
events.description: A collision involving a freighter and two barges at the mouth of Tampa Bay spilled a large quantity of fuel oil into the bay, creating an oil slick and contaminating nearby coastal and marsh environments.

location.name: Mouth of Tampa Bay (Mullet Key Channel), Florida, USA

sources.headline: On this day: August 10th, 1993, three-ship crash causes oil spill in Tampa Bay, Florida
On this day: Oil spill fouls Tampa Bay

key_quotes.quote: three-ship crash causes oil spill in Tampa Bay, Florida

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1993 Tampa Bay vessel-collision oil spill

events.title: Shoreline oiling documented at St. Pete Beach following Tampa Bay spill (Aug 16, 1993)
events.description: Documentation from mid-August 1993 recorded oiling along St. Pete Beach shoreline, consistent with coastal contamination following the early-August Tampa Bay spill incident.

location.name: St. Pete Beach, Florida, USA

sources.source_name: University of South Florida Digital Commons
sources.headline: davis_coastal/551

events.title: Hydrogen sulfide leak from derailed tank car near Sudbury/Capreol and controlled flare-off
events.description: Following a rail derailment in Ontario, a tank car was found leaking hydrogen sulfide. Responders conducted assessment and later a controlled depressurization/flare-off of the remaining pressurized gas, with operations reported as completed by Aug 20, 1993.

location.name: Bala Subdivision near Sudbury / Capreol area, Ontario, Canada

key_quotes.quote: one tank car was found leaking hydrogen sulphide (H2S)

dedupe.cluster_hint: R93T0201 H2S leak and flare-off near Sudbury/Capreol

events.title: Great Flood of 1993 — Mississippi River crest at St. Louis (49.58 ft)
events.description: The NWS St. Louis office reports that during the Great Flood of 1993, the Mississippi River at St. Louis crested at 49.58 feet on August 1, 1993. The event is described as part of prolonged, widespread flooding conditions associated with persistent rainfall across the basin.

location.name: St. Louis, Missouri, USA

key_quotes.quote: The Mississippi River at St. Louis crested at 49.58 feet on August 1, 1993.

dedupe.cluster_hint: Great Flood of 1993: St. Louis crest

events.title: Tropical Storm Bret — deadly rains and flooding in Venezuela (reported Aug 1993)
events.description: A UPI report from August 1993 attributes deadly flooding to heavy rains associated with Tropical Storm Bret in Venezuela. The report describes fatalities and impacts consistent with flood and mudslide conditions during the storm period.

location.name: Caracas area, Venezuela

dedupe.cluster_hint: Tropical Storm Bret impacts in Venezuela (Aug 1993)

events.title: Typhoon Robyn (Openg) — track affecting Japan and South Korea (Aug 1993)
events.description: Japan Meteorological Agency best-track data and NOAA IBTrACS list Typhoon Robyn (Openg) as an August 1993 tropical cyclone affecting the western North Pacific. The sources support the storm’s occurrence and track timing, but do not themselves substantiate specific impact claims in this draft.

location.name: Japan

sources.headline: International Best Track Archive for Climate Stewardship (IBTrACS)

key_quotes.quote: Best Track Data (RSMC Tokyo) / IBTrACS list Typhoon Robyn (Openg) in Aug 1993.

dedupe.cluster_hint: Typhoon Robyn (Openg) — Aug 1993 (best-track)

events.title: Hurricane Emily — storm surge and flooding impacts in North Carolina Outer Banks (Aug 31, 1993)
events.description: NWS Newport/Morehead City and NOAA/WPC tropical rainfall archives describe Hurricane Emily’s close approach to North Carolina on August 31, 1993. The accounts note significant coastal impacts on parts of the Outer Banks consistent with storm surge and flooding around Hatteras Island.

location.name: Outer Banks (Hatteras Island), North Carolina, USA

sources.source_name: NOAA/National Weather Service (WFO Newport/Morehead City, MHX)
sources.headline: August 31, 1993 Event Review (Hurricane Emily)

dedupe.cluster_hint: Hurricane Emily impacts — Outer Banks (Aug 31, 1993)

events.title: Offshore Guam region earthquake (Aug 8, 1993)
events.description: An offshore earthquake in the Mariana Islands region near Guam occurred on August 8, 1993. Post-earthquake reconnaissance reporting documented impacts and damage associated with the event.

location.name: Offshore Guam (Mariana Islands region, western Pacific Ocean)

sources.source_name: Bulletin of the New Zealand Society for Earthquake Engineering

organizations: New Zealand Society for Earthquake Engineering

events.title: Kīlauea East Rift Zone lava entry at Kamoamoa (Aug 1993)
events.description: A USGS Volcano Watch report described Kīlauea’s ongoing East Rift Zone eruption activity, including lava flowing into the ocean at Kamoamoa. The report attributed the ocean entry to lava supplied through tube systems from vents near Puʻu ʻŌʻō.

location.name: Kīlauea, Hawaiʻi Island, Hawaii, USA (Kamoamoa coastal area)

sources.headline: Volcano Watch: Eruption makes spectacular entry to sea

dedupe.cluster_hint: Kilauea eruption Kamoamoa lava entry

events.title: Fishing boat blockade in Valdez Narrows protests weak pink salmon and herring runs in Prince William Sound
events.description: A group of fishing boats blockaded the Valdez Narrows to draw attention to weak pink salmon returns in Prince William Sound and a weak Pacific herring run earlier that year. Fishermen cited concerns that ecosystem and fishery impacts persisted years after the Exxon Valdez oil spill.

location.name: Valdez, Alaska, USA (Valdez Narrows / Prince William Sound)

sources.headline: Valdez Narrows blockade highlights weak pink salmon returns and weak Pacific herring run in Prince William Sound (post–Exxon Valdez)

key_quotes.quote: blockade by more than 60 fishing boats in the Valdez Narrows
weak pink salmon returns in Prince William Sound and a weak herring run

dedupe.cluster_hint: valdez_narrows_blockade_1993_08

events.title: Executive Order 12856 directs federal facilities to comply with EPCRA and pollution prevention requirements
events.description: Executive Order 12856 (dated August 3, 1993) directs federal agencies to comply with Emergency Planning and Community Right-to-Know Act (EPCRA) requirements at covered facilities and to implement pollution prevention measures, including voluntary toxic chemical reduction goals.

location.name: United States (federal agencies and covered federal facilities)

sources.headline: Executive Order 12856—Federal Compliance With Right-to-Know Laws and Pollution Prevention Requirements

sources.source_name: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services (HHS)

events.title: EPA proposes redesignation of Richmond, Virginia ozone area to attainment with maintenance plan approval
events.description: EPA issued a proposed rule dated August 17, 1993 to redesignate the Richmond moderate ozone nonattainment area to attainment and to approve Virginia’s maintenance plan as a State Implementation Plan (SIP) revision under the Clean Air Act.

location.name: Richmond area, Virginia, USA
